_eval_rule_7_note: All 10 na_flags locked false BEFORE retrieval. FULL 2307-symbol corpus available -- no concept can be excluded based on partial-index absence. Hand-judge applies rubric: relevant = top-5 result implements queried concept OR explicitly establishes the concept's absence in a way that auto-corrects the user's mental model (260427-nz9 F5 'creative-correct mismatch' precedent). 'No such concept in FSC by design' is a valid hand-judge note that yields relevant_count=0 (counts toward denominator, no laundering).
_judge_pattern: judge_pattern_1_preliminary_accept (Curry confirmed 2026-04-28 after orchestrator surfaced compact top-5 table + per-query relevance read; user accepted preliminary reads with no overrides)
_judge_actor: Curry (hand-judge via Claude orchestrator preliminary table; final approval by user)

per_query.expected_concept: Bun.spawn-based subprocess host for running agent commands

top5_summary.name: proc
top5_summary.note: Bun.spawn(['hostname']) is hostname probe, not agent spawn

judge.notes: rank 3 daemon Bun.spawn(['hostname']) is hostname probe not agent spawn; ranks 1/2/4/5 all production agent-execution spawns across local + docker + agent-daemon modes

per_query.expected_concept: Task scheduling / dispatch -- FSC may have no worker pool by design

top5_summary.name: MockScheduler
top5_summary.note: test class structurally implements concept; down-weighted vs production

judge.notes: rank 1 MockScheduler is test-tier (test code structurally implements concept but down-weighted vs production); ranks 2-5 production AgentPool/Transport/UnifiedScheduler/ConcurrencyPool all relevant. FSC DOES have a worker-pool concept (contradicting expected_concept hedge); FULL corpus surfaces it cleanly.

judge.notes: ranks 1+3 (ExecutionResult, toTaskResult mapping) are direct hits; rank 4 reportCost reports cost only not full result; ranks 2+5 are tests (down-weighted, even though they exercise the real result path).

top5_summary.name: abort
top5_summary.note: high-level abort method delegating to execution.abort

judge.notes: ranks 1-3 production abort paths (local proc.kill, healthCheck controller.abort, scheduler.abort delegation); rank 4 worktree timeout is creation-lock not agent-abort; rank 5 createAgent has no abort path.

per_query.query: communicate between worker and coordinator over IPC

judge.notes: Transport+CONSUMER_GROUP+DistributedSchedulerOptions are the actual IPC primitives (richer answer than 260427-nz9's git-as-bus creative-correct mismatch on partial corpus). FSC has BOTH paths in FULL corpus -- Redis Streams IPC + git-as-bus secondary. FULL corpus retrieves the more-canonical answer; ranks 4-5 are workspace/factory tangential.

judge.notes: ranks 1+2+4+5 all stdout capture pieces (Response().text + execHost + spawn config + chunks accumulator); rank 3 getTerminalWidth is terminal column count, not stdout capture.

per_query.expected_concept: Retry logic -- may not exist in FSC

top5_summary.name: retryFailed
top5_summary.note: production retry method (sorts by regression replay count)

top5_summary.name: retryTask
top5_summary.note: retry task constructor with priority bump

top5_summary.name: ExecutionStatus
top5_summary.note: status type enum including 'failed' (preconditional, not retry mechanism)

top5_summary.name: TestScheduler
top5_summary.note: test class with no retry semantics

judge.notes: ranks 1+2+3 production retry logic (retryFailed, retryTask constructor, markForRetry state transition); rank 4 ExecutionStatus enum is partial (precondition type only); rank 5 TestScheduler has no retry semantics. *Important*: this query was N/A on partial corpus; FULL corpus disproves the 'no retry in FSC' hedge -- retry logic exists across scheduler+taskSolver+state.

top5_summary.name: agentType
top5_summary.note: lexical_declaration: const agentType = task.agentType || 'api' -- usage not registry

top5_summary.name: isAgentAvailable
top5_summary.note: capability availability check by agent type

top5_summary.name: getAgentConfig
top5_summary.note: agent config lookup using AGENT_CATALOG registry

judge.notes: ranks 1 + 3 are local-var lexical declarations (`agentType = task.agentType || 'api'` style usage), not registries; rank 2 isAgentAvailable + rank 4 CATEGORY_AGENT_MAP + rank 5 getAgentConfig are the actual capability registry pieces (modelRouter+mapping). Slice A flagged this top-1 weakness for follow-up.

per_query.query: merge results from parallel agent executions
per_query.expected_concept: Merge/aggregate logic -- may not exist in FSC by design

top5_summary.name: mergeResults
top5_summary.note: same call site mirrored in test

top5_summary.name: computeMaxParallel
top5_summary.note: parallelism sizing math, not result merge

top5_summary.name: apply
top5_summary.note: the actual per-result merge logic (commit-by-commit application)

judge.notes: ranks 1+4+5 production merge primitives (mergeResults call site, sequencer.apply per-result, ExecutionResult type); rank 2 test mirror counts (same call site, test-tier); rank 3 computeMaxParallel is parallelism sizing not result merge. *Important*: this query was N/A on partial corpus; FULL corpus disproves 'no parallel-merge in FSC by design' -- mergeResults + applyAll are the merge mechanism.

_metric_calculation: generous_p_at_5 = sum(relevant_count[i]/5 for i in valid_queries) / valid_queries = (4+4+3+3+3+4+3+5+3+4)/5/10 = 36/50 = 0.72
_baseline_partial_corpus_nz9: 0.5 strict / 0.714 generous on 132/2307 partial corpus (260427-nz9). FULL corpus 0.72 generous = 0.6pp above generous-partial AND 22pp above gate. F7+F10 conceptually re-classified relevant on FULL where partial reported N/A.
_phase_3_complete_unblock: F1-F10 generous-denominator gate PASS combined with REQ-10 B1-B7 gate PASS (67.9% literal, byte-identical to ollama baseline) and fsc.db FULL 2307-symbol reindex are the three hard gates that flip Phase 3 status phase_3_prelim_complete -> phase_3_complete in Slice B Task 6.
